text: Our parents take most of the decisions for us
comments: Shows limited agency of young women; parents make major life decisions.

text: we must give our opinion at least in all matters
comments: Indicates girls can express opinions but final authority not theirs ? nuanced decision-making power.

text: we don’t keep quiet; we speak up when we think we should
comments: Shows increasing voice among girls ? nuanced agency.

text: the final decision is always theirs
comments: Confirms male/parental authority despite girls voicing opinions.

text: we can think of making a career at school (teacher)
comments: Clear occupational aspiration for teaching.

text: Education is the key to a better life
comments: Education linked to life aspiration but not specific job.

text: We can have a better future if we are educate
comments: Education seen as pathway to future success.

text: Boys study till class 12th and girls till class 10th
comments: Shows gender gap in education attainment.

text: Girls should also be allowed to study till class 12th
comments: Expresses support for equal education.

text: They should be allowed to pursue college and technical education too
comments: Aspirations for higher education and technical careers.

text: we have to discontinue our studies since there is no college
comments: Structural barrier preventing education continuation.

text: travelling to the nearest college is difficult due to lack of transport
comments: Mobility + infrastructure barrier causing aspiration gap.

text: boys are given a preference because boys will take care of parents
comments: Shows gender bias still exists in some households.

text: Girls do all agricultural activities like planting, weeding and harvesting
comments: Opposes strict division of labor since girls do all activities.

text: Due to poverty
comments: Poverty is major barrier to education continuation.

text: Due to safety concerns for girls
comments: Safety concerns restrict girls’ education.

text: Because parents need extra hands at farm
comments: Household labor demand causes dropout.

text: Because of other problems at home including need to take care of younger siblings
comments: Domestic responsibilities cause girls to stop schooling.

text: I wanted to be a teacher
comments: Direct occupational aspiration.

text: Most of us work as farm labor
comments: Traditional livelihood outcome.

text: Girls should engage in stitching and handicrafts
comments: Income-generating aspiration.

text: We can also give tuitions to younger children
comments: Local income aspiration through teaching.

text: Women don’t work with machines
comments: Women excluded from mechanized agriculture knowledge.

text: Women cannot drive
comments: Reinforces norm men are better suited for machinery/farming.

text: People will laugh at them
comments: Social stigma if women use machines.

text: Young women do have the same opportunities but many don’t want to learn
comments: Suggests opportunity exists but uptake differs.

text: Young men have more exposure
comments: Men have more exposure to information.

text: both do same agricultural activities except land preparation
comments: Division exists mainly for heavy work.

text: young women are not allowed to go even to the local market
comments: Strong mobility restriction.

text: She will consult her husband
comments: Women must consult husband for farming decisions.

text: She will also consult her father
comments: Women consult male authority figures.

text: He will not let her join any such group
comments: Women restricted from groups due to gender norms.

text: He will fear that she may become corrupt
comments: Moral stigma used to restrict women’s mobility.

text: He will not let her sell vegetables
comments: Women restricted from selling in market.

text: Mark will a laughing stock
comments: Men doing women’s roles face stigma.

text: People will say he is a woman
comments: Social stigma for gender norm violation.

text: Today girls can be found in every profession
comments: Perception of improving equality.

text: Gender equality is important for the society
comments: Supports gender equality.

text: Women take this decision
comments: Women decide home garden consumption decisions.

text: David is more likely to sell the vegetables because it is a man’s job
comments: Confirms gender division in selling.

text: It be difficult for her to go against her husband’s wishes
comments: Women cannot make independent financial decisions.

text: She may be scared that he would dump her
comments: Threat of abandonment used as control.

text: Men do whatever they want to do
comments: Confirms male authority.

text: Girls don’t go to the market
comments: Mobility restriction.

text: Married women have the license to roam around alone
comments: Mobility increases after marriage ? nuanced.

text: It depends upon her husband whether he allows her or not
comments: Mobility controlled by husband.

text: It will be tough since that is also a male dominated place
comments: Market dominated by men restricts women.

text: Women can move around freely but only during daytime
comments: Mobility conditional ? nuanced.

text: No, girls cannot go outside the village
comments: Migration restriction.

text: we discontinue our studies since we are not allowed to go the city
comments: Mobility restriction causes aspiration gap.

text: Girls get married at 18-19 and boys at 24-25
comments: Unequal marriage age shows inequality.

text: we have no say in our marriage
comments: Confirms lack of agency in marriage decisions.

text: I want a man with a government job
comments: Marriage aspiration linked to economic security.

text: Boys and girls should meet and decide whether they want to marry
comments: Supports joint decision-making.

text: No they are too young to have babies at this age
comments: Supports delayed childbirth.

text: Women should have their first child at 23-24
comments: Supports later childbirth age.

text: she cannot decide when to have her first child
comments: Women lack reproductive decision-making power.

text: husband and wife should together take such decisions
comments: Suggests joint decision-making ideal.

text: We should both be allowed to grow like city youth do
comments: Aspirations for more freedom and opportunities.

text: we wish we also had such an environment
comments: Indicates lack of freedom compared to city girls.

text: We should have enabling education infrastructure
comments: Aspirations for education infrastructure.

text: police patrolling so that women don’t feel unsafe
comments: Safety concerns restrict mobility/work.
